title: logania scabrella
intro: Logania scabrella, also known as the scrub logania, is a flowering plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to eastern Australia. Logania scabrella has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Logania scabrella is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are brewed from the leaves and flowers of the plant to harness its medicinal properties.
